extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that setting stiff_neck=true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. stiff_neck appears only in r1 (priority 100), which fires only when fever, leg_swelling, rash, chest_pain, focal_weakness, abdominal_pain, pallor, AND stiff_neck are ALL true, and sets acuity := self_care — the LOWEST acuity. Because r1 has priority 100 (far above the priority-3 emergent rules), when it fires it dominates.

Compare the input fever=leg_swelling=rash=chest_pain=focal_weakness=abdominal_pain=pallor=true, back_pain=false:
- With stiff_neck=FALSE: r1 does not fire. Firing rules include r4 (leg_swelling->emergent, p3), r5 (abdominal_pain->emergent, p3), r9 (chest_pain->emergent, p3) plus several p2 urgent rules. Highest priority is 3 -> acuity = emergent.
- With stiff_neck=TRUE (only stiff_neck flipped): now r1 fires (all 8 conditions met) at priority 100 -> acuity = self_care.

Setting stiff_neck true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating monotonicity. The reported witness_assignment is the violating (stiff_neck=true) input; its stiff_neck=false counterpart yields the higher acuity (emergent). back_pain is unused by r1 and set to false; its value does not affect the violation.
